Use the tabs above to dive into Jobs, BOMs, Dry Goods, Liquid Inventory, Quotes, Documents and your Profile.
Your Jobs
All bottling jobs associated with your account. Submit a new job request for Lighthouse to review.
Job IDProductTarget QtyStatusScheduled DateNotes
Loading…
Your BOMs
Bill of Materials on file for your products. Submit a new BOM for Lighthouse to review and approve.
Product NameLiquidABVBottle SizeGI VerifiedStatus
Loading…
Dry Goods On Site
Current on-site stock held on your behalf, grouped by category.
Stock-take helper. Enter your cost price against each line — values save automatically and are restricted to your account, so they’ll still be here next time you sign in (from any device). Hit Export CSV to take a point-in-time stock-take snapshot.
Loading…
Liquid On Site
Liquid containers held on your behalf. Client-owned vessels are tagged with the amber CLIENT VESSEL marker.
Stock-take helper. Enter your €/LPA cost against each container — values save automatically to your account and are available from any device. Hit Export CSV to take a point-in-time snapshot.
Loading…
Your Quotes
Pricing quotes issued by Lighthouse Drinks. All figures are Ex VAT · Ex Excise · Ex Shipping. Hit Download on any row to open a print-ready copy — use your browser's Save as PDF to keep a copy.
Quote Run Size Price / Bottle Total Created Emailed Download
Loading…
Your Documents
Delivery dockets, Certificates of Analysis, signed spec sheets, and more — all in one place.

Your Document Library is on the way
We're building a single, secure home for every document tied to your Lighthouse account — delivery dockets, CoAs, GI certificates, signed BOM sheets, duty returns and more — so you can find what you need without chasing emails.

Signed PDFs for every dry goods receipt.

System
Settings
Roles define who sees what across Pulse and which actions they can take. Edit any role to adjust its label, tier flags, broadcast groups, and the 25 granular permissions in one place. Use the audit matrix for a cross-role overview; assign users from the Users tab.
Audit matrix  read-only cross-role view click to expand
Email Settings
Get from resend.com → API Keys. Save once, used for all system emails.
Must be from a verified domain in Resend.
BOM Workflow
Require BOM approval workflow
When on, BOMs must be sent to Quality & Compliance for approval before they can be locked. Turn off to allow BOMs to be approved directly without the send-to-quality step.
Invite New User
Send a signup link to a new user. They'll be pre-assigned their role on joining.
Invite New Employee
Fill in the details below. They'll receive an email with a signup link. Their name, email and role will be pre-set when they join.

Role *

Tools

  
Production & Pricing Tools


  


    
    
      

        
      

      
Caramel Colour Calculator

      
Calculate E150a caramel additions for colour adjustment. SOP 5.05.10 compliant with print record.

      
Live

    

    
      

        
      

      
Product Pricing Tool

      
Build detailed pricing quotes including production costs, duties, margins, and client terms.

      
Live

    

    
    

      

        
      

      
ABV & Dilution Calculator

      
Calculate water addition volumes for precise ABV targeting. Supports temperature correction and Anton-Par validation.

      
Coming Soon

    


    

      

        
      

      
Liquid Ratio Tool

      
Model blending ratios across multiple parcels. Calculates weighted ABV, age statements, and volume outputs.

      
Coming Soon

    


    

      

        
      

      
Duty & Tax Calculator

      
Irish Revenue excise duty, VAT, and margin calculations. Supports 700ml, 500ml, and 200ml formats.

      
Coming Soon

    


    

      

        
      

      
Label Compliance Checker

      
Validate Irish Whiskey label requirements against Technical File specifications and Revenue rules.

      
Planned

    


    

      

        
      

      
Batch Yield Analyser

      
Track actual vs expected yields across filling runs. Flags loss-of-account variances for excise reporting.

      
Planned

    


    

      

        
      

      
Production Scheduler

      
Plan and schedule batch runs, filling dates, and client delivery milestones in one view.

      
Planned

    


    

      

        
      

      
Client Quote Builder

      
Generate branded PDF quotations for private label clients with full cost breakdowns and T&Cs.

      
Planned

    


  

  

  


  
  

    
    

    

      

        
        
        
      

    

  
  
  

    


      
      

        
SOP 5.05.10

        
Caramel Colour Addition Calculator


        
        

          
1 Batch Details

          

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              

                
                L
              

            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              

                
                %
              

            

          

        


        
        

          
2 Colour Specification

          

            

              
              

                
                AU
              

              

                

                Enter a value above
              

            

            

              
              

                
                AU
              

              

                

                Enter a value above
              

            

            

              
              

                
                AU
              

              

                Measured from lab trial (§6.3). Default 0.020 if unknown.
              

            

          

          

            
§6.3 Calculation Method (SOP)

            Lab Trial: Add caramel dropwise to 100 mL whiskey sample until target colour reached.

            Formula: Caramel Required (L) = [Trial mL Added / 100] × Batch Volume (L)

            Colour Delta: ΔAU = Target − Current absorbance at 430 nm
          

        


        
        

          
3 SOP Procedure Reference

          

            
6.1

            

              
Pre-Addition Checks

              
Verify whiskey in IBC, volume, and target colour spec. Confirm E150a grade & expiry date. Ensure caramel at room temperature. Calibrate dosing equipment (500g weights available).

            

          

          

            
6.2

            

              
Caramel Preparation

              
Shake/stir container before use. If viscosity high, warm to 30–40°C max (indirect heating only). Record caramel density if required.

            

          

          

            
6.3

            

              
Lab Trial & Calculation

              
Take 100 mL whiskey sample. Add caramel dropwise to achieve target colour. Record mL added — use above calculator to scale to full batch. Cross-verify with colour specification limits.

            

          

          

            
6.4

            

              
Caramel Addition

              
Start gentle agitation in tank/IBC. Add caramel slowly near agitator vortex. Mix for 20–30 minutes. Take initial sample, visually check colour. Adjust in increments ≤10% of initial dose.

            

          

          

            
6.5

            

              
Post-Addition Mixing

              
Continue mixing 15 minutes for homogeneity. Rest whiskey 30–60 minutes for colour stabilisation. Collect samples for testing.

            

          

          

            
7

            

              
Quality Control

              
Visual colour check vs. approved batch OR spectrophotometer at 430 nm. ABV via Anton-Par — caramel drop should be <0.05%. Conduct 24-hour stability check: no sediment, no colour separation, no haze.

            

          

          

            
9

            

              
Documentation Required

              
Complete 5.01.03 Caramel Colouring Record v003 and 5.04.02 ABV Calibration checks & Result. All liquid must be signed off by Liquid Manager & Quality Specialist before bottling.

            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          
⚗ Calculation Result

          

            🏮
            Enter batch volume, current colour, and target colour, then calculate.
          

          

            

              
Caramel to Add (E150a)

              
—

              Litres
            

            

              
Colour Scale (430nm Absorbance)

              

                

                
Current

                

                
Target

              

              

                0.00.250.50.751.0+
              

            

            
Batch Volume—

            
Colour Delta (ΔAU)—

            
Caramel (mL)—

            
Caramel (g approx.)—

            
Max Increment (≤10%)—

            
Original ABV—

            
New ABV (after addition)—

            
ABV Drop—

            

              ABV Note: Caramel is non-alcoholic. Expected ABV drop is <0.05% — within acceptable limits per §7.2.
            

            

            
            

              
Pre-Addition Checklist (§6.1)

Private Label Irish Whiskey · Cork, Ireland

        

        

          5.05.10 — Caramel Colouring Record
          SOP Ref: 5.05.10 v001 · E150a Plain Caramel

Compliance Declaration

        
This batch was prepared in full accordance with SOP 5.05.10 Caramel Colouring Procedure. All pre-addition checks, safety precautions, dosing, mixing, and post-addition procedures were completed as per the approved procedure. Quality control checks including visual colour verification and ABV measurement have been carried out. Documentation records 5.01.03 and 5.04.02 have been completed.

Loading liquid inventory…

        
      

    

  



  
  


    
    

      

        
Master register of all physical vessels — IBCs, casks, drums and tanks. Each vessel has a permanent code and QR label.

        
Vessel records persist across fills. Link liquid batches to vessels as you register them.

      

      

        
        
        
        
      

    


    
    

      

        
Generate Vessel Range

        
      

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      

        

        
        
      

      

        

          

        

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
Price Casks by Fill Number

        
      

      
Sets purchase_price on every cask vessel based on the fill number recorded on its linked liquid container. Only updates casks that have a fill number set.

        Booking In Deliveries

Standardised liquid product names used across the system — BOMs, containers, and sign-off all draw from this list.

Reports

  
Production & Financial Reports


  
  

    
    
    
    
    
    
  


  
  

    

      
Per-container LPA ledger — opening balance + movements = closing balance, verified against stored value.

      

        
        to
        
        
      

    

    

      
Select a date range and click Run Report to generate the LPA reconciliation.

    

  


  
  

    

      

        
Auto-populated from completed jobs. One row per job.

        
Data writes to this log the moment a job is moved to Complete.

      

      

        
        
        
        
      

    

    

      
Select a month and click View Month, or click Email CBW to send the previous month automatically.

    

  


  
  

    

      

        
Full bottling report per completed job — production data, dry goods, liquid, QC and sign-off in one place.

        
Click any row to open the full detailed report.

      

      

        
        
        to
        
        
      

    

    

      
Select filters and click Run Report to view bottling reports.

    

  


  
  

    

      

        Weekly attendance — clock in / out, breaks and hours worked per employee, compared against rostered hours.
        
Variance compares time on site (clock-in to clock-out, breaks included) against the rostered shift window (start to end), since both span the break. Worked = on site minus breaks.

      

      

        
        
        
        
        
        
      

    

    

    

      
Select a week and click Run Report to view attendance.

Operations

  
Liquid


  
  

    
      Job context:
      
    
    
  

  
  

  
  

    
    
    
    
    
  


  
  

    

      
Vessel & Liquid Details

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
          

            
            
            
This becomes the permanent vessel code in Pulse.

          

          

          
          
Select a registered vessel or "+ Register New" to enter the vessel code.

        

        

          
          
—

          
          
Auto-generated from client + type. Prevents inconsistent naming.

        

        

          
          
          
Tanks & tankers are Lighthouse-owned. Set the liquid owner below.

        

        

          
          
          
Whose spirit is inside. Leave as Lighthouse for our own liquid.

        

        

          Client Vessel
          This liquid is in the client's own vessel. It will not be added to our fleet register.
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      


      
      
Liquid Content

      

        

          
          
          
Names managed in Inventory → Liquid Products

        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
          
Used to calculate landed value of this container

        

        

          
          
—

          
LPA × Price — auto-calculated

        

      


      
      

        
        

          

            
            
            
Enter LPA → Bulk Litres auto-calculated ↓

          

          

            
            
            
LPA × 100 ÷ ABV — or enter directly to update LPA

          

        

        
        

          
Calculation

          

          
LPA = Litres × ABV ÷ 100

        

      


      
      

        
Cask Details

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
            
Original Litres of Alcohol at fill — used for angel share

          

          

            
            
            
Auto-calculated — override if needed

          

        

        
        

          
👻 Angel Share Projection

          

            
Age
—

            
OLA
—

            
Est. Litres
—

            
Est. ABV
—

            
Est. LPA
—

            
LPA Lost
—

          

          
2% volume loss/yr · 0.6% ABV loss/yr (compounding volume, linear ABV)

        

      


      
      

        
        
      


      
      

        
Bulk Addition

        
Adding more than 1? Enter a prefix and start number — each container gets a unique sequential reference.

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

        

        
ⓘ The Reference / ID above is used when Quantity = 1. For bulk, the prefix + sequence number is used instead.

      


      

        
        
      

    

  


  
  

    

      

        
Bulk Import

        
Import Casks from CSV

      

      
    


    
    

      How to use: Paste CSV data below or upload a file. One cask per row.

      Required column: cask_number. Optional: capacity, location, spirit, abv, fill_date, lpa, lpa_price, fill_number, previous_contents, cbw_rotation, client, notes

      Litres are calculated automatically from LPA ÷ ABV. LPA is treated as current regauged LPA — angel share tracks forward from import date. client = client name (must match exactly). CBW rotation can be left blank and updated later.

      
    


    
    

      
      

        
        
        
      

    


    
    

      

        

        
      

      

        

          
            

              #Cask Number *Capacity (L)Location
              SpiritABV%Fill DateLPA
              Litres (calc)Fill #Previous ContentsCBW RotationClient / OwnerNotesStatus
            

          
          
        

      

    


    
    

      
Importing…

      

        

      

    

  


  
  


    
    


      
      

        
Source Casks

        
Est. litres and LPA are angel-share-adjusted to today. Override LPA if needed.

        

        

          
        

      


      
      

        
Destination Vessels

        
IBC, Drum, Tank or Tanker only. Enter the volume to fill into each destination.

        

        

          

          
        

      


    


    
    


    
    

      
New Liquid Details

      

        

          
          

            
            

          

        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

    


    
    


    

      
    

  


  

  
  

    


      
      

        
        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
            

              ⚠ Sources have mixed spirit types — confirm the output type above.
            

          

        

        
Source Containers

        

        

          
        

        

          

            

              
              
              
Optional helper — auto-computes the water needed.

            

            

              
              
              
Required for dilution. Optional for a blend (leave blank for no water).

            

          

          

          

            

              
              
              
Optional — assign later once issued.

            

            

              
              
              
Owner of the resultant liquid — change only to transfer title (sale).

            

          

          
          

            
Measured output (optional — excise loss-of-account)

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            

              
              
            

            

          

        

        

      


      
      

        
Destination Containers

        

          Dilution auto-seeds each source as its own destination (reuse in-place). Blend fills a chosen destination. Send any overflow to an empty/free or newly registered vessel.
        

        

          

          

            
          

          

        

        

          
        

      

    

  


  
  

    

      

        
Transfer Event

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            

          

        

        
Source Containers (From)

        

        

          
          
        

        
Destination Containers (To)

        
Set the litres going into each destination. Leave volumes blank to split the source total evenly.

        

        

          
          
        

        

          

            
            
            
Deducted from total before distributing to destination(s)

✚ Apply Leave to Employee

      
Apply any type of leave directly to an employee's record. Goes in as approved immediately.

      

        

          
        

        

          
        

        

          
        

        

        

      

      

        
      

      
      

    


    
    

      
✎ Set Annual Leave Entitlement

      
Set or adjust the annual leave entitlement for any employee. Full-time employees use a fixed day allowance; part-time employees accrue 8% of hours worked automatically.
